brit: verb. To break in pieces; divide.

hip: noun. The outward-projecting parts of the pelvis and top of the femur and the overlying tissue.

hot: adjective. Relating to heat and conditions which produce it.
obi: noun. A sash worn with a kimono.

ort: noun. A fragment; a scrap of leftover food; any remainder; a piece of refuse.
phi: noun. Φ, the 21st letter of the Euclidean and modern Greek alphabet, usually romanized as "ph".
phot: noun. A photometric unit of illuminance, or luminous flux through an area (symbol ph).
pht: interjection. Used to signify mild annoyance or disagreement.
pit: noun. A hole in the ground.

poh: interjection. Indicating that something is trivial and not worth consideration.

port: noun. A place on the coast at which ships can shelter, or dock to load and unload cargo or passengers.
pot: noun. A flat - bottomed vessel (usually metal) used for cooking food, possibly excluding saucepans (see usage notes).
pro: noun. An advantage of something, especially when contrasted with its disadvantages (cons).
probit: noun. A unit, derived from a standard normal distribution, used in measuring the responses to doses.

rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.

rob: verb. To steal from, especially using force or violence.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
roti: noun. A kind of unleavened flatbread commonly consumed in South Asia and the Caribbean.
thorp: noun. A group of houses standing together in the country; a hamlet; a village.
thrip: noun. Optional singular for thrips, an insect of the order Thysanoptera.
throb: verb. To pound or beat rapidly or violently.
tip: noun. The extreme end of something, especially when pointed; e.g. the sharp end of a pencil.

trio: noun. A group of three people or things.
trip: noun. A journey; an excursion or jaunt.

boh: interjection. An exclamation used to startle or frighten.
